subject: Human Resources
title: What Is Human Resources?
description: A micro-lesson that makes What Is Human Resources? usable.

hook.text: Every company has people. Managing those people well is the difference between success and chaos.

buildup.text: Human resources is the department that manages hiring, training, pay, benefits, and employee relations.

discovery.text: HR exists because people aren't machines—they need support, fairness, and clear expectations to thrive.

twist.text: Good HR is invisible. Bad HR makes headlines—lawsuits, scandals, and mass resignations.

climax.text: Companies that invest in HR outperform those that don't. People are the ultimate competitive advantage.

punchline.text: HR manages the most complex asset: people.

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of human resources?
